channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hong Kong ex-publisher Jimmy Lai testifies he didn't ask Pence, Pompeo to take action against city
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Former Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai denied in his landmark national security trial on Wednesday he had asked then U.S.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The mausoleum holding Congo independence hero Lumumba's gold-capped tooth is vandalized
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s government says the mausoleum of assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba in the capital, Kinshasa, has been vandalized. It's not immediately clear whether Lumumba’s remains — a single gold-capped tooth — were damaged or stolen.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marcos says Indonesia has agreed to send Filipino death-row drug convict back to the Philippines
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. says a deal has been reached for Indonesia to send back a Filipino death-row drug convict, who was nearly executed by firing squad but got a reprieve due to years of pleadings from Manila.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez And Niniek Karmini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US envoy says Israel-Hezbollah truce is 'within our grasp' as Gaza food crisis worsens after looting
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A United States envoy said an agreement to end the Israel-Hezbollah war is “within our grasp” after talks in Lebanon on Tuesday. However, there was no such optimism in the Gaza Strip, where the looting of nearly 100 aid trucks by armed men worsened an already severe food crisis.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden draws support — and GOP worry — in shift on US missiles in Ukraine war
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are generally offering support for President Biden's decision to authorize a major shift in U.S. policy to allow Ukraine to use long-range U.S. missiles to attack sites in Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — As dawn arrived off the coast of Senegal, navy commander Assane Seye scanned the horizon.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Ukrainians want talks to end war with Russia: Gallup
item.description: More than half of Ukrainians want talks to end the war with Russia, according to Gallup polls released Tuesday, the war's 1,000th day.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The separatist leader of Georgia's breakaway region of Abkhazia resigned on Tuesday after days of unrest, in which the opposition and its supporters seized key government buildings and at least 14 people were injured in clashes with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists were sentenced to four to 10 years in prison Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Israel strike against Iran hit nuke program during October retaliatory strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that part of Iran's nuclear program was degraded after IDF strikes on military facilities in and around Tehran in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian troops train for trench warfare near France's WWI battlefields
item.description: Around 2,000 Ukrainian soldiers are training in France's eastern Marne region, where German and French armies battled each other during World War I.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire kills at least 10 people in Spanish retirement home
item.description: A fire that broke out in a retirement home in the Spanish town of Villafranca de Ebro has left 10 people dead; all of the victims were reportedly elderly people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
